creator: pneumann@gmail.com
text:
Hi Matthias,

Unfortunatly, i cannot reproduce the problem.

What asterisk version are you using?

Can you make sure that the files in grep
"\.h" /usr/ports/net/asterisk/pkg-plist
Are all okey?...
Or delete asterisk, make sure the files are wiped out and install it
again.


Ive seen similar things, when old .h files lied around.

thanks!
-- 
Phillip Neumann <pneumann@gmail.com>
